response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a technically accomplished individual contributor with over 15 years of software development experience and genuine developer tooling credentials — CI/CD setup, test framework authorship (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and MCP/agent infrastructure work. However, the resume contains no evidence of people management, formal team leadership, or organizational change ownership, all of which are hard minimums for this Level II Manager role. The career arc is unambiguously that of a senior IC, and without demonstrated experience conducting 1:1s, setting performance expectations, or leading headcount, the candidate does not meet the structural requirements of the position regardless of technical merit.
strengths: Genuine developer tooling depth: authored open-source test runners and assertion libraries, set up CI/CD infrastructure, and currently works on MCP code generators and agentic frameworks
Broad technical range across web, mobile (iOS/Apple TV), embedded, and distributed systems spanning 15+ years
Community knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, conference talks, and open-source project maintenance
concerns: No people management experience anywhere in the resume — no mention of direct reports, performance reviews, hiring, or team leadership
Staff-level titles held without apparent leadership scope; founder role at GISCollective is a solo open-source project, not an organizational leadership position
Mobile platform knowledge is shallow and dated — a brief iOS stint at WatchUp (2015–2016) does not satisfy the mobile-platform depth the role centers on
